description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked logs with visible interlocking corners, giving it a traditional and sturdy appearance. The cabin features a large, steeply pitched gable roof made of weathered wooden planks, extending over a small covered porch at the front entrance. The facade includes symmetrically arranged windows with wooden shutters, adding to its classic charm. The structure sits on a raised stone or concrete foundation, providing stability and protection from the ground. The side and rear views reveal additional windows, maintaining a balanced and functional design. The overall look is reminiscent of traditional log cabins found in forested or rural settings, but it could also serve as a unique residential building in an urban neighborhood.
description: A traditional log cabin with a steep gable roof, symmetrical shuttered windows, and a small covered front porch, built from stacked logs and set on a raised foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, centered entrance, and two shuttered windows on either side.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the gable roof, two symmetrically placed windows, and the log construction details.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the uninterrupted log wall and the expanse of the gable roof, with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, with two windows and the log wall construction clearly visible.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a steep pitch, constructed from wooden planks and featuring a moderate overhang
attribute_window_arrangement: Four medium-sized rectangular windows with wooden shutters, symmetrically arranged in pairs on each side wall; no windows visible on the rear wall
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade, sheltered by a covered porch
